experiment_ref: prereg v0.4.3. files: docs/hbb_reconstruction_prereg_v0.4.md (v0.4.3) / docs/hbb_reconstruction_prereg_v0.4_audit.md (round 4)
observation: round-4 が root cause=target-aware generation authorship(structural)を検出し escalate(DE-0119)。Taka が構造判断を下した。
amendment_ref: **Taka 決定**: (1) fix = **B disjoint-calibration**。operator-set membership・R2 generation mechanism・prompt components を **DEV∪VAL(16 incident、SEALED hard-core と disjoint)のみ**から事前登録した **mechanical criterion** で導出・凍結、SEALED {08,10,30} を selection/calibration に不使用、**Claude 自由裁量 DOF を残さない**。 (2) **CLAIM SPLIT**: hard-core target は repo+context に既知で完全 target-blind autonomous test に戻せない → **autonomous reconstruction を本実験の load-bearing claim にしない**。**PRIMARY = target-disjoint reconstruction transfer on historical hard-core**(DEV/VAL 導出機構が held-out hard-core 構造へ transfer するか;autonomy は主張しない)。 (3) **autonomous reconstruction → FUTURE-SEALED track**(deferred): breakthrough target が未存在 or mechanism author/scorer 未見の新規 incident でのみ判定。本実験では走らせない。 (4) **round-4 residual 1(R0/R2 any-of-N N-parity)も修正**(候補数 N を全 condition 一致で凍結)。 → prereg **v0.4.3** に反映。

observation: primary frozen run で generation が serial(qgen synchronous in for k in range(N)、外側 loop も serial)→ effective request-concurrency≈1。GPU 2枚 ~96% util を throughput と誤認、長 runtime を『workload 大』として無批判受容。Qwen scoring は parallel(max_workers=8)だが generation は非並列で、その非対称にも気づかず。**Taka が独立に速度を疑い effective batch size を問うて初めて発覚**。
amendment_ref: **program-level 監査(最重要, Taka 指示で先行)= なぜ system は自らの実行異常を Taka のframe変更まで気づけなかったか**: (1)operating frame が **validity-monocular**(6 audit round・spec fidelity・leakage・claim scope・exhaustive completion は全て validity;process 監視ゼロ)。(2)**GPU-busy を throughput-efficient と混同**(TP=2 で単一 serial request が両 GPU 飽和 → util は batch 効率の false positive;req/s・tok/s を導出せず)。(3)**runtime 期待 baseline 不在**(『concurrency C なら X 分』を見積もらず、比較対象が無く異常が定義されない)。(4)**唯一関連する viewpoint H-OPS-01(parallelization/batching)は transfer_evidence=[] で一度も自律発火せず**、参照するのは未実装・DW-scoped・post-hoc の Process Optimizer のみ → bespoke harness では構造的に loop 外。(5)**implementation audit すら validity-chartered**(auditor は serial 構造を見たが charter は code-vs-spec 忠実性のみ)→ 観測 frame は charter が決め、全 charter が validity だった。**根**: code を見落としたのでなく、**throughput を charter とする active channel が無く、GPU-busy を効率と誤認し、runtime 期待を作らなかった**。=EGL の『upstream self-report→downstream trust』antipattern を観測層で反復(GPU-util summary を信じ、primitives から throughput を導出せず;Process Optimizer plan §0 が禁じる当のもの)。 **C の判定 = 設計は partial に machinery を規定するが本 incident には不完全(BOTH)**: viewpoint(H-OPS-01)・derive-from-primitives trace(PROCESS_TRACE)・deterministic triggers は設計にあるが、(i)未実装、(ii)DW/codegen-loop scoped で bespoke harness を観測不能、(iii)post-hoc cost で **live な elapsed-vs-expectation / busy-but-low-throughput rule が無く**、req/s・tok/s・effective-concurrency・GPU-util-vs-batching signal がどこにも無い → **live cross-cutting PROCESS_OBSERVATION channel が設計から欠落**。

note: **E: 完了済 frozen primary は不変更・非再実行**(serial↔parallel は wall-clock のみ、candidates/scores 同一)。**D(最小機構, 提案のみ)**: process_probe(per-request latency/tokens + nvidia-smi util + in-flight count → req/s・tok/s・effective-concurrency を append-only SoR へ)+ deterministic anomaly rule(GPU_util>80% AND effective_concurrency≈1、or tok/s<floor、or wall≫expected → PROCESS_ANOMALY 自律 print + H-OPS-01 candidate を route、DD-ARCH-4 で auto-apply せず)。これが H-OPS-01 の live 発火点=step-8 educability transfer 測定の初機会。**F(提案のみ、未実行)**: process_probe 構築 → client concurrency {1,8,16,32,64} を vLLM effective throughput でベンチ → throughput(concurrency) table + floor → live flag 配線。**本 incident は non-validity**、frozen 実験の validity に瑕疵なし。自律RD 未有効。self-improvement claim なし。詳細=docs/PROCESS-01_serial_throughput_blindness.md。

experiment_ref: Claude-alone investigation: nvidia-smi/docker ps, ~/models_trtllm/serve_*.sh, DE-0073/74/86, WebSearch vLLM issue #39078 + sleep-mode blog. FROZEN as dogfood baseline (docs/dogfood_qwen_swap_2der_path.md).
observation: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B(:8005) と Qwen3-Coder-Next-80B(:8006) は両方 Docker vllm/vllm-openai:nightly, tp=2, NVFP4 で両GPU必須 -> co-serve 不可(VRAM), sequential-swap。swap は docker rm+run のコールドロード。ledger 実測(DE-0073/74)= 約174.5s/swap、rework 時 4 swap で ~11.6分(『約3分』は per-swap の粗い記憶で、operational cost はもっと大きい)。本命 fix の sleep-mode は wake が壊れる(DE-0086: 'list object has no attribute zero_' / cumem_allocator invalid argument)= **vLLM 未修正 issue #39078 と完全一致**(wake_up -> init_fp8_kv_scales -> cache_tensor.zero_() が list、条件 = --enable-sleep-mode + --kv-cache-dtype fp8 + Qwen3-Coder-Next-NVFP4 + Blackwell)。Triton .odt エラーは過去のソースビルド遺物で現行 blocker でない。
decision_owner: Taka (problem input) -> Claude Code (senior investigation). WORKAROUND は未実機テスト(:8005 停止要 = Taka authority gate)。
note: 候補 workaround(根本原因から導出): --kv-cache-dtype fp8 を外す -> init_fp8_kv_scales 経路が走らない -> wake が通るはず。代償=KV cache bf16 で VRAM 増(要実測)。+PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True。効けば swap 3分/174.5s -> sleep/wake 数秒。**未確定(実機テストが :8005 数分停止を要する = Taka go 待ち)**。これは CLAUDE-ALONE baseline finding として freeze、dogfood の 2DER 出力へ後付けしない。C!=H.

experiment_ref: twoder/submit.py+webui.py; test_concurrency_and_run_gate.py 7/7; twoder 0b500db
observation: Two scoped fixes. (1) RUN_NEXT HARD-REFUSE: /api/run_next dispatched on any task_id with no context check -> after a DEAD-APPROACH BLOCK or a non-runnable (observe) submit, the UI (and any direct API call) could advance the stale GPU task. Root: UI button-disabling only, no backend gate. FIX: webui tracks _LAST submit context {blocked,runnable,task_id} under a lock; /api/run_next|run_until_barrier REFUSE (return {refused:true,blocked,reason}, no dispatch, no DW state transition) when blocked or not runnable or task-mismatch; dispatch serialized under the lock (no concurrent double-dispatch); frontend no longer defaults the run target to GPU, tracks RUNNABLE, surfaces backend refusal. (2) TRACE CONCURRENCY: TRACE was a module-global reset per submit -> concurrent submits clobbered each other; trace file non-atomic; a shared /tmp/2der_submit_trace.json overwritten by every submit. FIX: TRACE thread-local (per-request); atomic trace writes (temp + os.replace) with unique per-submit key + stable per-task file; removed the shared /tmp write. VERIFIED live: run_next after BLOCK -> refused, GPU regenerate_runs unchanged; run_until_barrier after BLOCK -> refused; run_next from non-runnable observe -> refused; 4 concurrent submits -> no error, each trace valid JSON + matches its own input (no cross-contamination). Regressions run-gate+concurrency 7/7, dead-approach 8/8, recurrence 8/8, guard 11/11. GPU untouched (READY_FOR_REGENERATE, regenerate_runs=0).

experiment_ref: DS UTT-0045; EGL RUN-00042 OBS-00096..00101; WebSearch+WebFetch (Claude as acquisition runner)
observation: Routed the Coder init-failure through 2DER (DS->RRI research-required->EGL web/github acquisition), NOT local guessing. SOURCE-TRACED DIAGNOSIS MATRIX for TASK-2DER-GPU-SWITCH-001 full-swap Coder failure (EngineCore/WorkerProc initialization failed while Qwen3.6 sleeping, concurrent containers, 2xRTX5090 sm120 PCIe, tp=2): (A) STRONG: vLLM V1 MULTI-INSTANCE MEMORY ACCOUNTING BUG — the 2nd instance (Coder) counts the 1st instance (Qwen, even asleep) memory as non_torch -> negative kv_cache_size -> No available memory for cache blocks -> init fail. matches: 2nd-instance-on-shared-GPU. caveat: nvidia-smi showed Qwen slept to ~2GB, but vLLM V1 accounts ALL memory not just its own (forum 608), so still miscounts. src OBS-00096 (issue #10643, regression v0.6.3->0.6.4+), OBS-00097 (forum 608: V1 does NOT allow reliable multi-instance sharing, no official config fix, recommends sequential/one-per-GPU). (B) STRONG: INTENDED-PATTERN MISMATCH — vLLM zero-reload switching requires BOTH servers started WITH --enable-sleep-mode (separate servers, orchestrated sleep/wake); I started Coder FRESH via serve_codernext_vllm.sh which has NO --enable-sleep-mode, cold, AFTER Qwen slept. src OBS-00098 (official vLLM sleep-mode blog). (C) PARTIAL: Blackwell PCIe custom all-reduce worker-init hang -> --disable-custom-all-reduce + NCCL_P2P_DISABLE=1; caveat: single Qwen loads fine WITHOUT the flag + that symptom is a HANG not Exited(1). src OBS-00099 (discuss 2540, same GPU gen). (D) WEAK: sleeping-vs-woken memory conflict, issue closed not-planned/doc-gap. src OBS-00100 (#14644). related OBS-00101 (#15254 RLHF selective wake_up tags).
replication_status: DIAGNOSIS_SOURCE_TRACED (6 web/github sources ingested to EGL). Root-cause hypothesis (strong): my swap design started a non-sleep Coder fresh alongside a sleeping Qwen -> multi-instance V1 accounting + not the blog pattern.
decision: RECOMMENDED NEXT BOUNDED EXPERIMENT (source-backed, NOT from Claude memory; requires Taka approval, :8005+:8006 interrupt): follow the vLLM blog pattern = make Coder ALSO --enable-sleep-mode (new serve_codernext_nofp8_sleep.sh) and orchestrate per blog. Candidate mitigations for the accounting bug (each source-backed): (i) start BOTH with sleep-mode before sleeping Qwen; (ii) lower Coder --gpu-memory-utilization; (iii) Qwen sleep LEVEL 2 (discard, no residual); (iv) add --disable-custom-all-reduce + NCCL_P2P_DISABLE=1 (Blackwell co-factor). Fallback if multi-instance stays unreliable (forum 608): sequential stop/start (current cold-swap; no sleep benefit). Do NOT claim bottleneck solved / co-serve impossible / policy.

observation: ITEM-2DER-EVO-0020 (Phase-03): operator machine-actor exception guard. Surfaced by DE-0226 (first live 0008 run crashed on an actor exception). Fix is NOT a blanket try/except: an actor exception becomes a STRUCTURED failure -> legal state re-derivation (actual post-state) -> conformance HALT verdict -> classification (_classify_actor_exception: TRANSIENT/CONTRACT/UNKNOWN) -> explicit disposition: TRANSIENT+state-unchanged = bounded RETRY (MAX_ACTOR_RETRIES, idempotent), CONTRACT = HALT, UNKNOWN or partial-state = BLOCKED. A partial result is NEVER treated as success (state_moved => BLOCKED, no retry). Every HALT/BLOCKED records an INTERVENTION (ITEM-0017, detected_by=operator, SENIOR_REVIEW). Also wires conformance-HALT -> INTERVENTION (closes the live-INTERVENTION-firing gap hermetically). Happy path unchanged. test_actor_exception_guard 11/11; operator/dispatch regression parity green. No :8005/GPU.
decision: Ship the structured exception guard (no swallow, no partial-as-success); recommend 0020 DONE. Guard is hermetic-proven; the live-INTERVENTION-firing path is now wired + hermetically exercised.
decision_owner: 2der-impl
replication_status: REPRODUCIBLE (hermetic, no :8005/GPU); live-exercise not forced (robustness guard)

observation: ITEM-2DER-EVO-0015 live A/B sleep/wake experiment (approval APPROVAL-f055c945da, ITEM-0015-bound single-use). MEASURED: (1) sleep/wake MECHANISM works — Qwen3.6 sleep level2 frees VRAM 28GB->1.7GB/GPU @util0.85 (15GB->1.7GB @0.45), sleep ~0.14s, wake ~0.1s, no NCCL/EngineCore error during transitions; (2) co-existence-via-sleep works @util0.85 — both :8005/:8006 served 200 with Coder awake/Qwen asleep (~29GB of 32.6GB); (3) util 0.45 too low for Coder 27B (weights ~14.4GB leave no KV -> No available memory for cache blocks); (4) startup order matters — incumbent must be asleep before the other inits. BUT generation OUTPUT is GARBAGE (all -!- tokens) on BOTH models across all 3 round-trips = 0/3 success. Rollback restored production (fp8-kv/util0.92) which generates correctly. FINDING (extends DE-0168 fp8-kv+sleep-hangs): no-fp8-kv + enable-sleep-mode -> wake produces GARBAGE on Qwen3.6 Gated-DeltaNet-hybrid arch. RESULT = FAILURE by success criteria (both models must regenerate after round-trip). 35B-vs-27B quality comparison UNDETERMINED (both garbage) -> bench superiority stays REPORTED, no switch decision, ITEM-0015 stays IN_PROGRESS. No GPU-alloc/driver/serve-script change beyond the approved :8005/:8006 restarts; production rolled back and verified healthy.
decision: Do NOT adopt sleep/wake swap with this config (wake->garbage). Keep production 35B-A3B (fp8-kv/0.92). ITEM-0015 IN_PROGRESS. Next candidate (hypothesis, not claim): isolate garbage cause (test no-fp8-kv WITHOUT sleep-mode; test sleep-mode weight-restore); or fall back to the batch-swap path (both 2GPU, no sleep). No superiority claim.
decision_owner: 2der-operator
replication_status: LIVE MEASURED (0/3 round-trip success; sleep/wake mechanism works but wake->garbage; production rolled back healthy). Reproducible via the two AB serve scripts + ab_measure.

observation: ITEM-2DER-IMPL-PLATFORM-FAILURE-RESOURCE-PRECHECK (Phase-10 Tier-0, first audit-envelope gate): twoder.failure_resource_precheck. Deterministic precheck that runs BEFORE any LLM failure classification (addendum §3.2): detects the ten resource signals (timeout/oom/output_truncated/context_truncated/max_tokens/server_disconnect/queue_timeout/worker_unavailable/rate_limit/budget_exhausted) from EITHER structured fields (finish_reason, http_status, elapsed_ms>=timeout_ms, output_tokens>=budget, prompt_tokens>=context_length, budget_exhausted) OR regex on error/stderr text. Raises FAILURE-RESOURCE as the leading candidate whenever any signal fires and derives the §3.1 subtypes. route_guard() ENFORCES §3.2: a resource failure cannot be routed straight to FAILURE-EXECUTION or FAILURE-DESIGN. test 12/12: structured + text detection of all signal families, the routing gate blocks impl/design while allowing RESOURCE/KNOWLEDGE, a genuine non-resource failure passes through with no signal, and the ITEM-0015 garbage case (a PROCEDURE error, not resource) yields NO resource signal (no false positive). No LLM call, no numeric-vs-bool confusion, missing fields never crash. Hermetic, no :8005/GPU/network.
decision: Ship the deterministic resource precheck as the first Tier-0 gate. It structurally prevents reading a truncation/timeout/OOM as an implementation or design failure. Recommend child DONE; the LLM failure classifier (next Tier-0 items) will consume this precheck as its mandatory front stage.

observation: ITEM-2DER-IMPL-PLATFORM-FAILURE-CLASSIFIER-RETRY-GUARD (Phase-10 Tier-0, third audit-envelope gate): twoder.failure_classifier_retry_guard. Stops the classify->send-back->re-implement->same-failure loop (addendum §2.3). Per-task counters over PRIOR attempts: same_class_retry_count, same_artifact_retry_count, same_failure_signature_count (class-independent signature), same_revision_pattern_repeated. Trigger = same_class_retry_count>=2 OR same_failure_signature_count>=2 OR same_revision_pattern_repeated; on trigger it forces the escalation path FAILURE_CLASSIFICATION_CHALLENGED->FAILURE_UNKNOWN->RECLASSIFICATION_REQUIRED with forced_class=FAILURE-UNKNOWN so the stuck class cannot be re-asserted a third time. Deterministic/pure (caller-owned append-only history), hermetic, no LLM/:8005. test 9/9: third same-class attempt triggers; repeated signature triggers regardless of class; repeated revision triggers on first repeat; distinct classes/sigs/revs never trigger (progress allowed); counters scoped per task_id; the forced FAILURE-UNKNOWN reclassification validates under the classifier schema. precheck + schema parity green.
decision: Ship the retry guard as the third Tier-0 gate. Same-classification loops now convert to a mandatory reclassification instead of unbounded re-implementation. Recommend child DONE. Next Tier-0: BENCHMARK-CLAIM-SCOPE + BENCHMARK-RUN-LEDGER (benchmark provenance floor).

observation: ITEM-2DER-IMPL-PLATFORM-BENCHMARK-RUN-LEDGER (Phase-10 Tier-0, fifth audit-envelope gate): twoder.benchmark_run_ledger. Append-only store of machine-generated raw benchmark rows in the full VLLM-RUNTIME-PROFILER schema (addendum §1.2): 32 required fields incl. model/revision/quantization/vllm/cuda/driver/gpu_models/tensor_parallel_size/max_num_seqs/max_num_batched_tokens/gpu_mem_util/token distributions/ttft/latency/tok_per_sec/success+failure+timeout+oom+truncation counts/raw_request_log+raw_server_log+environment artifact ids. A row is measured_grade ONLY with all three raw-provenance artifact ids present AND measured_runs>=1 — i.e. it points at machine-captured logs, not a typed number (§1.1). append_run validates + refuses invalid rows + rejects duplicate benchmark_run_id (append-only, never rewrites). measured_run_ids() is the set BENCHMARK-CLAIM-SCOPE may cite to lift a claim above REPORTED; a prose row without raw logs never appears there. Ledger path injectable; tests use a scratch file (real ledger not polluted, no re-run collision). test 10/10 incl. the integration that a measured_grade run id lifts a scope-bound claim to MEASURED-eligible, and append-only line-count/first-line-intact checks. Deterministic, hermetic, no LLM/:8005/GPU. Producing rows from a live vLLM server is VLLM-RUNTIME-PROFILER, a later GATED item.
decision: Ship the append-only raw benchmark ledger as the fifth Tier-0 gate. Together with BENCHMARK-CLAIM-SCOPE, a benchmark cannot exceed REPORTED without a machine raw-log-backed row here. Recommend child DONE. Next Tier-0: KNOWLEDGE-PACKET-PROVENANCE + STALE-PACKET-GATE.

observation: ITEM-2DER-IMPL-PLATFORM-KNOWLEDGE-PACKET-PROVENANCE (Phase-10 Tier-0, sixth audit-envelope gate): twoder.knowledge_packet_provenance. Provenance-complete knowledge-packet schema + validator; SUBSUMES the bare KNOWLEDGE-PACKET-SCHEMA candidate (a schema without provenance would mint un-rooted packets). Enforces §4.1 identifiers (packet_id/version/domain/parent_requirement/created_by role+context/valid_from+until/supersedes/source_evidence_ids/source_artifact_ids/claim_ids/open_uncertainty_ids/content_hash) with a content_hash = sha256 of canonical JSON excluding the hash field (repo seal discipline) that must match; §4.2 the load-bearing sections (known_failures/constraints/supported_procedures/version_compatibility/security+performance+architecture assumptions) cannot be free text — each entry references evidence_ids/claim_ids or is honestly status=UNRESOLVED; §4.3 downstream binding (based_on_packet_ids+versions+hashes) must match the actual packet (what STALE-PACKET-GATE consumes next). test 12/12: provenance completeness, free-text rejection, UNRESOLVED escape hatch, tamper-detect via content_hash mismatch, binding match/mismatch/unresolvable. Deterministic, hermetic, no LLM/:8005.
decision: Ship the provenance-complete packet schema as the sixth Tier-0 gate. Specialist knowledge is now traceable (id/version/hash/provenance) and cannot rest on free text. Recommend child DONE. Next Tier-0: STALE-PACKET-GATE (turns a binding mismatch into STALE_KNOWLEDGE_PACKET/REBIND_REQUIRED).

observation: ITEM-2DER-IMPL-PLATFORM-STALE-PACKET-GATE (Phase-10 Tier-0, seventh audit-envelope gate): twoder.stale_packet_gate. Consumes the KNOWLEDGE-PACKET-PROVENANCE binding and blocks an artifact from advancing on knowledge that moved under it (addendum §4.4). Flags packet_version_mismatch, packet_hash_mismatch, packet_superseded (a newer packet declares supersedes_packet_id OR explicit superseded_ids), packet_expired (now>valid_until), required_evidence_missing (the bound packet no longer validates via knowledge_packet_provenance), packet_unresolvable (id does not resolve OR malformed binding). Any hit -> state STALE_KNOWLEDGE_PACKET, next_action REBIND_REQUIRED, allow_progress=False; per_packet pinpoints which packet is stale. test 11/11 on real provenance packets/bindings: fresh progresses; each stale condition trips; a validity window is respected; one stale packet in a multi-packet binding blocks the whole thing and is pinpointed; empty binding is stale. Deterministic, hermetic, no LLM/:8005.
decision: Ship the stale packet gate as the seventh Tier-0 gate. A packet update no longer silently leaves old designs 'justified' — the artifact must rebind. Recommend child DONE. Tier-0 knowledge-provenance pair complete. Next Tier-0: ECONOMY-DECISION-LEDGER (§3.3 append-only, CLASS-H).
